# Handover Memo (2026-03-26)

This memo summarizes the latest viaduct-side handling changes, persistence updates, and follow-up checks.

## Completed Today

### 1) Auto-Generated Elevated Side Handling in Erase Mode

- Viaduct erase selection now supports both:
  - manual viaduct wall lines (VCT)
  - auto-generated elevated track side lines
- Delete behavior for auto-generated sides no longer removes track geometry.
- Instead, per-track side suppression flags are used:
  - `sideDisabledPositive`
  - `sideDisabledNegative`

## 2) Save/Load Persistence Revision

- Save schema bumped to version `11`.
- Goal: keep auto-generated sides reproducible from tracks, not duplicated as persisted wall geometry.
- Manual VCT walls are explicitly persisted with `origin: "manual"`.
- Legacy saves are normalized:
  - if old `viaductWalls` entries match auto-generated track sides, they are removed during load migration.

## 3) Regression Fix for Gradient Tracks

Issue observed after viaduct persistence changes:

- gradient track centerline appeared visually broken (clipped)
- false dead-end bars appeared near clipped sections

Fixes applied:

- Gradient segments are now excluded from higher-viaduct clipping range logic.
- Dead-end markers are skipped for endpoints currently hidden by higher viaduct areas.

## Files Touched in This Cycle

- `app.js`
  - save serializer / schema version update
  - legacy viaduct wall migration filter
  - manual wall origin tagging
  - gradient visibility range fix
  - hidden-endpoint dead-end suppression
- `README.md`
  - schema version and migration notes update
- `save/transitnexus-2026-03-20-08-50-01.json`
  - sample save version updated to 11

## Important Current Behavior

- Auto-generated elevated sides are runtime-derived from track geometry.
- Their deleted state is persisted via track side-disabled flags.
- Manual VCT walls remain fully selectable/deletable and persist as manual walls.
- Legacy data containing auto-generated wall artifacts should be cleaned on load.

## Recommended Verification Next Session

1. Draw Lv1+ and gradient tracks, save/load, confirm side lines regenerate consistently.
2. Delete one auto side on a track, save/load, confirm deleted side remains suppressed.
3. Verify manual VCT lines still save/load and delete independently.
4. Confirm no gradient clipping or false dead-end bars in viaduct overlap scenes.

## Notes

- Repository currently also has many `node_modules/.bin/*` modified files in working tree.
- Those are not part of feature logic and should generally stay out of commits unless explicitly intended.
